title: Friction Diagnostics Event
description: Canonical schema for friction event records. A cognitive debugging tool for agent behavior — captures WHY an agent made decisions and where reasoning diverged from reality.

event_id.description: Unique event identifier (evt-NNNN), auto-generated sequentially per events file

recorded_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of event creation

fingerprint.description: Content-based hash for recurrence detection. Default: hash(source_ref|date). Override with --fingerprint-key for intentional clustering.

title.description: Short human-readable event summary; derived from actual_outcome when omitted

events_file.description: Canonical path to the events.jsonl file this event belongs to

repo_root.description: Absolute path to the repository root

expected_outcome.description: What the agent expected to happen — the contract or assumption that was violated

actual_outcome.description: What actually happened — verbatim error message, exit code, or output. Never paraphrase.

reading.description: Full first-person story: what the agent consulted, did, observed, interpreted, and why it made the decisions it made. This is the core value — it traces the agent's reasoning chain so that instructions and environments can be improved.

hindsight.description: What the agent would do differently knowing what it knows now. About the agent's decisions, not fixes to source material.

ref.description: File path, URL, or reference string

excerpt.description: Verbatim quote from the source — the specific text the agent acted on

sources.description: Where friction originated. Each entry points to a source the agent consulted or acted on.

impact.description: How the friction affected the agent's ability to continue. blocked=work stopped, degraded=workaround used, noisy=extra retries/effort, continued=no workflow disruption.

tags.description: Specific, natural classification tags as-filed. Normalized to lowercase on write. Example: ["ssh-auth-sock", "git-signing", "1password"]

aliases.description: Broader grouping tags for classification. Normalized to lowercase on write. Example: ["auth", "git"]. Query tool searches both tags and aliases by default.
